titlestmt.titleproper: Finding aid for the Dan McLaughlin drawings and animation materials LSC.2360
titlestmt.author: Finding aid prepared by Jasmine Jones, 2019.

p.date: Online finding aid last updated 2026 April 14.

address.addressline: Room A1713, Charles E. Young Research Library

publicationstmt.p: Finding aid description and metadata in this record are licensed under a Creative Commons Public Domain Designation (CC0 1.0 Universal). See LSC's Metadata Rights Statement for more information.

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2026-04-14 10:46:26 -0700.

did.unittitle: Dan McLaughlin drawings and animation materials

physdesc.extent: (1 unprocessed carton, 2 unprocessed flat boxes, 1 unprocessed tall record carton, 1 unprocessed oversize flat file)

did.abstract: Dan McLaughlin was head of the UCLA Animation Workshop for 37 years, from 1970 until his retirement in 2017, and founder of the MFA Animation Program. His portfolio contains works of traditional, digital, and experimental animation, as well as numerous academic articles on these and other topics. This collection is comprised of sketches and original cels completed by the animator in collaboration with numerous studios, including Jay Ward, Hanna Barbera Productions, Murakami/Wolf Productions, and Chuck Jones Productions. Credits include: Smoke the Bear, Yogi Bear, Scooby Doo, The Flintstones, The Pink Panther, Tom and Jerry, How the Grinch Stole Christmas, An American Tale, and various animated commercials.
did.physloc: Held at UCLA Library Special Collections. Advance notice is required for access to the collection. All requests to access special collections materials must be made in advance using the request button located on this page.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Unprocessed collection. Material is unavailable for access. Please see LSC's Access to Unprocessed Collections Policy for more information about levels of discovery and access of special collections materials and registering interest in unprocessed collections.

acqinfo.head: Provenance/Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of Maura McLaughlin Weber, Loren McLaughlin, and Dan McLaughlin, Jr., 2018.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: [Identification of item], Dan McLaughlin drawings and animation materials (Collection 2360). UCLA Library Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, University of California, Los Angeles.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived user interest and research value, availability of staff and resources, and competing priorities. Library Special Collections provides a standard level of preservation and access for all collections and, when time and resources permit, conducts more intensive processing. These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards and best practices.

We are committed to providing ethical, inclusive, and anti-racist description of the materials we steward, and to remediating existing description of our materials that contains language that may be offensive or cause harm. We invite you to submit feedback about how our collections are described, and how they could be described more accurately, by filling out the form located on our website: Report Problematic Content and Description in UCLA's library collections and archives.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Dan McLaughlin was head of the UCLA Animation Workshop for 37 years, from 1970 until his retirement in 2017, and founder of the MFA Animation Program. His portfolio contains works of traditional, digital, and experimental animation, as well as numerous academic articles on these and other topics.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: This collection is comprised of sketches and original cels completed by the animator in collaboration with numerous studios, including Jay Ward, Hanna Barbera Productions, Murakami/Wolf Productions, and Chuck Jones Productions. Credits include: Smoke the Bear, Yogi Bear, Scooby Doo, The Flintstones, The Pink Panther, Tom and Jerry, How the Grinch Stole Christmas, An American Tale, and various animated commercials.

physdesc.extent: (1 unprocessed carton, 2 unprocessed flat boxes, 1 unprocessed tall record carton, 1 unprocessed oversize flat file)
